title: GMS Week 35 - SNORING SEPTEMBER?
summary:
Main
As August winds down, global markets gave us another week of shy half-gestures and newlywed hesitant moves, all while confidentlypretending they were marching somewhere important. Oil traders kept one eye on inventories, the other on geopolitics, and both cockeyed on the clock as WTI closed at USD 64/barrel with Brent just above USD 68-moves so small they looked like rounding errors. Yet the steady draw of about 3 million barrels from U.S.
